channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: FBI employees questioned about involvement in Jan. 6 cases as Justice Department weighs firings
item.description: Thousands of FBI employees who participated in investigations related to the Jan. 6, 2021, riot at the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Eric Tucker And Alanna Durkin Richer, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Rubio says Panama must reduce Chinese influence around the canal or face possible US action
item.description: PANAMA CITY (AP) — U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio told Panamanian President José Raúl Mulino on Sunday that the Central American ally must immediately reduce Chinese influence over the Panama Canal area or face potential retaliation from the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE and JUAN ZAMORANO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Watch: Fans boo US national anthem at NHL game in Canada
item.description: The booing came shortly after Trump signed an order imposing stiff tariffs on imports from Canada, Mexico, and China.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's second week in office delivers jolts and chaotic orders with a mix of politics and tragedy
item.description: President Donald Trump’s second week in office seemed to deliver a daily dose of deliberate jolts for the country.
item.dc:creator: Michelle L. Price, Chris Megerian And Will Weissert, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Democrats elect Ken Martin, the party leader in Minnesota, as their national chair
item.description: OXON HILL, Md. (AP) — Democrats on Saturday elected Ken Martin, the party leader in Minnesota, as the national chair, turning to a low-profile Midwestern political operative to coordinate their resistance to Donald Trump’s presidency.
item.dc:creator: STEVE PEOPLES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Army releases name of 3rd soldier who died in helicopter and airliner crash
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Army on Saturday released the name of the third soldier who died Wednesday when an Army helicopter collided with an American Airlines jet near Reagan National Airport in D.C. Cpt. Rebecca M. Lobach, of Durham, N.C., served as an Army aviation officer since July 2019.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US military conducts airstrikes against Islamic State operatives in Somalia
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The U.S. military has conducted airstrikes against Islamic State operatives in Somalia, the first attacks in the African nation during President Donald Trump’s second term. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said Saturday that the strikes by U.S.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, AAMER MADHANI and OMAR FARUK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Democrats elect new chair who branded Trump a 'traitor' as party aims to rebound from disastrous 2024 election
item.description: The Democratic National Committee (DNC) on Saturday elected Ken Martin as its next party chair, in the wake of major setbacks up and down the ballot in the 2024 elections.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump fires the director of the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau
item.description: President Donald Trump has fired the director of the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau, Rohit Chopra, in the latest purge of a Biden administration holdover.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump wants to cut the federal workforce. Who they are and what that means
item.description: President Trump’s efforts to cut the federal workforce by 10 percent might not save as much money as hoped and could have unintended consequences. NPR explains why.
item.dc:creator: Scott Neuman, MPR News

item.title: Patel dodges, demurs as Democrats press him on controversial statements
item.description: Kash Patel, President Trump’s pick to lead the FBI, wouldn’t acknowledge or sought to distance himself from a host of his past public statements as Democrats pressed him during his confirmation hearing on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Kash Patel grilled on potential Trump pressure, past comments at contentious hearing
item.description: Kash Patel, President Trump’s nominee to lead the FBI, was at the center of a contentious confirmation hearing Thursday, facing intense grilling from Democrats on the Senate Judiciary Committee about his past comments and allegiance to Trump.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: More migrants deported under Biden, Baja state official says
item.description: Tijuana Mayor Ismael Burgueño Ruiz says he will not lift an emergency declaration he made last month as a way to prepare for mass deportations from the United States.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, Fox 5

item.title: Lawmakers clash over immigration policies, in-state tuition for Dreamers
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (WFLA) — As Gov. Ron DeSantis and legislative leaders in Tallahassee clash on immigration proposals, DeSantis said he's planning to veto the recently passed TRUMP Act. That move could put lawmakers back to square one.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Gabbard battles senators over Snowden, Syria in fiery hearing: 5 takeaways
item.description: Tulsi Gabbard, President Trump’s pick to be the next director of national intelligence, endured a difficult hearing before the Senate Intelligence Committee on Thursday as lawmakers pressed her on her views on Edward Snowden, spying, Syria and Russia.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's pick for intel chief, Tulsi Gabbard, faces her confirmation hearing
item.description: Among the topics likely to come up at the hearing: her 2017 meeting with Syrian President Bashar al-Assad, her friendly positions toward Russia and her push to drop charges against Edward Snowden.
item.dc:creator: Jenna McLaughlin, MPR News

item.title: Trump's FBI director pick, Kash Patel, faces confirmation hearing
item.description: Kash Patel, President Donald Trump's pick to run the FBI, answers questions Thursday in a public hearing before the Senate Judiciary Committee.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Lucas, MPR News

item.title: Trump says U.S. will send 'worst criminal illegal aliens' to Guantánamo Bay
item.description: The president says up to 30,000 criminal migrants deported from the United States could be housed at the facility in Cuba, but it wasn't immediately clear how the plan would be implemented.
item.dc:creator: Sacha Pfeiffer, MPR News

item.title: DeSantis signals veto of TRUMP Act, criticizing lawmakers' immigration bill
item.description: After a late night for lawmakers in Tallahassee, Governor Ron DeSantis says his veto pen is ready to reject the TRUMP Act.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump orders Education, Labor and other departments to enhance school choice
item.description: The president directed several departments to submit plans for using federal funds to expand school choice.
item.dc:creator: Jonaki Mehta, MPR News

item.title: Idaho House calls on Supreme Court to undo same-sex marriage ruling
item.description: The Supreme Court does not overturn its previous rulings absent a new case presenting a legal challenge.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: Proposed trims to Minnesota’s disability waiver program concern people reliant on services
item.description: In his new budget, Gov. Tim Walz recommends restraining growth in the state’s disability waiver program — one of the best funded of its kind in the country. That has sparked worry despite Walz assurances that access and quality won’t suffer.
item.dc:creator: Peter Cox, MPR News

item.title: Trump signs Laken Riley Act, marking first legislative win of second term
item.description: President Trump on Wednesday signed the Laken Riley Act into law, marking his first legislative victory since he returned to the White House after he campaigned on immigration and the border during his 2024 campaign.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Trump administration to offer buyouts to all federal workers ahead of return to office
item.description: The Trump administration is offering all 2 million federal employees what amounts to a buyout if they do not intend to return to work in person later this year, sources confirmed to The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Florida House and Senate leaders take charge in special session showdown
item.description: Florida Governor Ron DeSantis pushed for a special session to help boost President Donald Trump's immigration efforts. But that session was blocked by Senate and House leadership Monday.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Nevada Republicans pass Democrats in voter registration, PAC reports
item.description: Nevada Republicans said Monday they have passed Democrats in voter registration in the state for the first time since 2007.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: NJ congressman launches plan to tackle rising meat and egg prices
item.description: FORT LEE, N.J. (PIX11) -- Food prices in the coming year are expected to continue to increase, according to the Department of Agriculture. It's unlikely to be the sharp rise we saw in the past few years, but certain products such as eggs and meats continue to rise more than most other groceries.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Trump's move to freeze Biden-approved funding draws howls from Democrats
item.description: Democrats are sounding the alarm over President Trump's move to freeze funding approved in two of former President Biden's signature laws. Trump issued an executive order pausing the disbursement of funds appropriated through the Inflation Reduction Act and the Bipartisan Infrastructure Law.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5
